message:
fix(fr-command): CLIENT KILL/LIST ID + KILL SKIPME error wording matches upstream [bd-frankenredis-lkoh]

Three small CLIENT subcommand wording divergences from upstream
Redis 7.2:

- CLIENT KILL ID abc / CLIENT KILL ID 0 / CLIENT LIST ID -1:
  fr previously emitted "ERR Invalid client ID"; upstream
  networking.c::clientCommand emits "ERR client-id should be
  greater than 0" for both unparseable and non-positive IDs.

- CLIENT KILL SKIPME BAD: fr previously emitted "ERR argument
  must be 'yes' or 'no'"; upstream emits the generic
  "ERR syntax error".

- CLIENT KILL LADDR <ip:port>: previously fell through to
  the generic SyntaxError. Upstream accepts LADDR and returns
  Integer(0) when no clients match. Since fr does not track a
  distinct laddr per client at the dispatch layer, the filter
  cannot match any session — return Integer(0) directly.

Updates two affected fr-command tests to the new wording.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
